paper_title: Reconstruction of Tropical Conditions and Climate Models

methods.method_name: Pollen Analog Method for Tropical Influence Reconstruction
methods.description: This method uses pollen analogs to establish the potential growth of mangrove trees at examined pollen sites, aiming to reconstruct the relative tropical influence at these sites.

steps.description: Surface analogs from four mangrove sites in South Texas, North Mexico, and Central Florida were sampled using ten pinch samples of 1 cc volume separated by a minimum distance of 1 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface analog samples from mangrove sites
inputs: mangrove sites in South Texas, North Mexico, Central Florida

methods.description: This method involves drawing pollen diagrams and performing zonation using cluster analysis to group pollen spectra into stratigraphic zones.

steps.description: Pollen diagrams were drawn using the TILIA program.
Zonation was performed using a stratigraphically constrained incremental sum of squares cluster analysis according to percentage data, facilitated by the CONISS program.
steps.searchable_summary: perform zonation using CONISS cluster analysis

methods.description: This method provides chronological control by dating bulk sediment samples using AMS and standard radiocarbon techniques.

steps.description: Bulk sediments were screened for rhizomous, indeterminate wood and indeterminate molluscan or gastropod remains in a 177-micron brass screen.
Pre-processed sediments were mailed to the Beta Analytic Radiocarbon Laboratory for dating.
steps.searchable_summary: send sediment samples for radiocarbon dating

methods.method_name: Climate Model Analysis for Forest-Limit Oscillations
methods.description: This method uses climate models to define mesic and xeric climate phases based on shifts in the forest-prairie ecotone and arboreal pollen data.

steps.description: Mesic periods were reconstructed according to increased AP in the Guadalupe River valley and Copano Bay.
steps.searchable_summary: model mesic periods using AP data
inputs: AP data from Guadalupe River valley and Copano Bay

steps.description: Xeric periods were defined by AP values below 22.4% at all examined sites.
steps.searchable_summary: define xeric periods using low AP values
